nameMeanings.name: Pelham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Sussex): habitational name from Brent Pelham, Furneux Pelham, and Stocking Pelham in Hertfordshire, so called from the Old English personal name Pēola + hām ‘homestead’. Compare Pellham .

History: The manor of Pelham in Hertfordshire, England, was held by Walter de Pelham in the reign of Edward I (1272–1307). His descendants became constables of Pevensey Castle, Sussex, and were so influential that their badge, the buckle, is seen in at least eleven of the county's churches, and as a decoration on iron chimney-backs in Sussex farmhouses. Various branches of the family were ennobled and their titles include earl of Chichester and earl of Yarborough. The family also once held the dukedom of Newcastle and the marquessate of Clare. Peter Pelham (born c. 1695), an engraver, emigrated to Boston, MA, after 1728, and was stepfather to the artist John Singleton Copley.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Pelham Esq. was born about 1425, in Laughton, Sussex, England, his father, Sir John Pelham Knt., was 39 and his mother, Joan Courcy, was 28. He had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Margaret Edmonds. He died on 1 February 1516, in his hometown, at the age of 92, and was buried in Laughton, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Pelham Esq. est né environ 1425, à Laughton, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Pelham Knt., avait 39 ans et sa mère, Joan Courcy, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Margaret Edmonds. Il est décédé le 1 février 1516, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 92 ans, et a été enterré à Laughton, Sussex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Pelham Esq.が約1425年にイギリススセックスラウトンで生まれた時，父Sir John Pelham Knt.は39歳，母Joan Courcyは28歳であった。彼はMargaret Edmondsとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1516年2月1日に故郷で92歳で死去し，イギリススセックスラウトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Pelham Esq.(이)가 대략 1425년 영국 수세스 로그h턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Pelham Knt.(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Courcy(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Margaret Edmonds(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1516년 2월 1일 그의 고향에서 92세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 로그h턴에 묻혔다.
